item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Heh, it's about time the CotBG gave something a god complex with how they obsess over potential Mekhane pieces.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Man, this is really cool and I love it. Every part of this article fits together well, and the CotBG implementation manages to tread the thin line between unjustified jamming in and overcomplicated deep lore impressively well. The logic used to justify the god complex is simple in the best way and really manages to tie this all together. Strong upvote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
While I am doubtful about the effectiveness of such scripture to a megalomanic god-machine, this is still gold. Or at least a metal worthy of MEKHANE.

So when's that comment rating system coming?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I think everyone else has already handled positive feedback. A thought:

One could shoot down the god complex with this refutation (here written as if it was addressed to 3179):

Assume declarations 2-4 are correct.

Two things which share a property are not necessarily the same thing.

Therefore, it is indeterminate whether or not you are the MEKHANE.

Therefore, it is indeterminate whether or not you are a god.

Additionally, humans do not necessarily need to obey the rulings of gods.

Therefore, you will not be released.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
A deranged metal alien with a god complex. What more can you possibly ask for?
+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I bloody love this. An alien machine that was accidently given a false god complex. Helluva concept with a great execution. Enthusiastic +1!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I'm shocked to see the Broken Church and Foundation working together, but this convinced me they would. There've been a lot more cooperative GoI skips of late.

What makes this work is the ways it gets considerably more insidious in its escape attempts by the end. I mean, the screaming and so forth is pretty neat, but at this point, you can't trust that anyone is safe from infection.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I could clearly imagine the heated discussions about whether or not this was a divine manifestation or not. My headcanon depicts the Mekhanists as elderly British steampunk Thelemites, arguing in quavering Home County accents while, in the background, desperate acolytes try to contain the thrashing metal behemoth.

I'd upvote just for giving me that image, and yet you did even more.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Fixed the MTF designation, thank you for pointing it out!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
A false prophet for the tickers. I love it. This is so incredibly neat.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
dumb nitpick: Zeta-9 is the designation for the Mole Rats

other than that, this is a cool idea and i like the logical advances in the thing's intelligence

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like this as a sort of homage to older sci fi/horror films if this is what it's going for. Even if it wasn't it reminded me of Alien and films like it so +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: According to Ihp, it's pronounced 'Mek-Ha-Ney', you heathen (though I personally prefer the two-syllable pronunciation myself).

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is a cool combination of the T-1000, the Color Out of Space, and Broken God theology, and the tablets are a surprisingly strong note to end on.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Let the Pagan spill theirs
O'er mountain, hill, and plain
They will be converted
To serve our lord MEKHANE


+1

item.title: Author Post
item.content:encoded:
Thanks to Gummy_Dragon, Westrin, sepia, Varaxous, PeppersGhost, and DarkStuff for their feedback!

PLEASE DON’T SUE TANHONY

The source for the image is (http://wiible.tumblr.com/post/45530779029/boiler-explosion-december-19-1890-in-wallingford). According to the source, the image was taken in 1890, and is therefore considered Public Domain in the United States.
